name: Almansa Park, Cabezo Gordo, Sima de las Palomas, Cueva del Agua – Ruta Circular – San Javier
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around San Javier offer a diverse landscape for cycling, combining coastal routes along the Mar Menor with more varied terrain inland. The region features flat, scenic paths through salt flats and nature reserves, alongside routes that explore geological formations like Cabezo Gordo. Riders can experience a mix of paved and dirt roads, providing options for different skill levels and preferences. Best mountain bike trails around San Javier The most popular mountain bike trail is Almansa Park, Cabezo Gordo, Sima de las Palomas, Cueva del Agua – Ruta Circular – San Javier, an easy 14.4 miles (23.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural area around Cabezo Gordo and Almansa Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Marchamalo Salt Flats – Cala de los Déntoles loop from Urbanización Los Miradores del Puerto, a moderate 34.5 miles (55.6 km) path. This route offers coastal views and passes through the Marchamalo Salt Flats. Local mountain bikers also love the La Calcetera Windmill – Molino de la Calcetera loop from Los Pescadores, a 15.9 miles (25.6 km) trail leading through the Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar Nature Reserve, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Mountain biking around San Javier is defined by coastal paths along the Mar Menor, salt flats, and inland hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more moderate distances. The routes in San Javier are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 280 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore San Javier's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around San Javier?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around San Javier listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes along the coast and inland, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in San Javier?
A: San Javier benefits from a warm climate, making it suitable for mountain biking year-round. Many routes, like the Transmurciana Route, remain open to the public throughout the year. The pleasant climate ensures comfortable riding conditions for most of the year.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in San Javier?
A: Yes, San Javier offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or family outings. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937116178/almansa-park-cabezo-gordo-sima-de-las-palomas-cueva-del-agua-ruta-circular-san-javier" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almansa Park, Cabezo Gordo, Sima de las Palomas, Cueva del Agua – Ruta Circular – San Javier&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.4-mile route that explores a natural area with unique features.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around San Javier?
A: The terrain around San Javier is quite varied. You'll find flat, scenic paths through salt flats and nature reserves along the Mar Menor, as well as routes that explore geological formations like Cabezo Gordo. Some trails also incorporate both paved and dirt roads, offering a mix of surfaces. For more challenging ascents and technical sections, areas like the Carrascoy y El Valle Regional Park offer mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in San Javier?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in San Javier are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937116178/almansa-park-cabezo-gordo-sima-de-las-palomas-cueva-del-agua-ruta-circular-san-javier" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almansa Park, Cabezo Gordo, Sima de las Palomas, Cueva del Agua – Ruta Circular – San Javier&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38263438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Calcetera Windmill – Molino de la Calcetera loop from Los Pescadores&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see while mountain biking in San Javier?
A: While mountain biking around San Javier, you can encounter several interesting landmarks and natural features. Routes often pass by the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/663908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de la Calcetera&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/620881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchamalo Salt Flats&lt;/a&gt;, and the protected &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt Flats and Sand Dunes of San Pedro del Pinatar&lt;/a&gt;. The prominent geological formation of Cabezo Gordo is also a notable feature on some routes.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in San Javier?
A: While many trails around San Javier are moderate, more challenging routes can be found in nearby areas. The Carrascoy y El Valle Regional Park, for instance, is known for its extensive chain of mountains, offering challenging ascents and technical descents, providing a significant test for experienced mountain bikers.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in San Javier?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in Spain, especially in natural parks, provided they are kept under control, ideally on a leash, and owners clean up after them. It's always best to check local signage or park rules for specific restrictions before heading out.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in San Javier?
A: The mountain bike routes in San Javier are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse scenery, which combines coastal paths along the Mar Menor with inland hills and natural reserves, offering a variety of experiences for different ability levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer coastal views in San Javier?
A: Absolutely. San Javier is situated on the Mar Menor, and many mountain biking trails offer stunning coastal views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33818957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchamalo Salt Flats – Cala de los Déntoles loop from Urbanización Los Miradores del Puerto&lt;/a&gt; provide scenic rides along the Mar Menor coastline and through coastal nature reserves.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in San Javier?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in San Javier varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937116178/almansa-park-cabezo-gordo-sima-de-las-palomas-cueva-del-agua-ruta-circular-san-javier" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almansa Park, Cabezo Gordo, Sima de las Palomas, Cueva del Agua – Ruta Circular – San Javier&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in under 2 hours. Moderate routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38255013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Path near Elche – Lovelo Coffee Ride loop from La Ribera - Avenida Virgen de Loreto, 149&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 3.5 hours.

Q: Are there options for mountain biking near natural parks in San Javier?
A: Yes, San Javier offers excellent mountain biking opportunities near natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38263438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Calcetera Windmill – Molino de la Calcetera loop from Los Pescadores&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through the Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar Nature Reserve. Additionally, the Calblanque Regional Park is accessible via routes like the Transmurciana Route, offering diverse landscapes.
tips: It is the largest park in the municipality of San Javier. As a decorative element, a beautiful structure of arches in its entrance and the long pergola stand out. There is plenty of parking and a restaurant.

The Sima de las Palomas is a 20 m deep karstic abyss in which numerous human remains have been found, the second oldest in the Iberian Peninsula. Sites have been found with hominid remains dating back to about 150,000 years ago.
The Cueva del Agua is an old mine exploited at the end of the 19th century of the 20th. Inside there is a freshwater lake and there are five different species of bats.
